examinar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for describing physical examinations: 'El médico examina al paciente' (The doctor examines the patient).

inyectar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb used when administering medications: 'Voy a inyectarle una dosis de antibiótico' (I'm going to inject you with an antibiotic dose).

prescribir.clinicalNote: Essential verb for medical professionals: 'El médico prescribe antibióticos para la infección' (The doctor prescribes antibiotics for the infection).

diagnosticar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb used to report clinical findings: 'Le diagnosticamos diabetes' (We diagnosed him with diabetes).

suturar.clinicalNote: Essential surgical verb used when describing wound closure techniques: 'El cirujano suturó la incisión con precisión.'

hidratar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in medical practice for patient care instructions: 'Hidrate al paciente cada dos horas' (Hydrate the patient every two hours).

monitorear.clinicalNote: Essential in medical contexts for continuous patient observation; e.g., 'Monitoreamos los signos vitales durante la cirugía' (We monitored vital signs during surgery).

evaluar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for documenting patient assessments: 'Evalúo los síntomas del paciente' (I evaluate the patient's symptoms).

reanimar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in emergency medicine; used when describing cardiopulmonary resuscitation procedures and patient recovery protocols.

tratar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb for describing patient care: 'Tratamos la infección con antibióticos' (We treat the infection with antibiotics).

operar.clinicalNote: Essential surgical verb; use present to describe procedures: 'El cirujano opera al paciente.' Use subjunctive in recommendations: 'Es necesario que lo operemos cuanto antes.'

ingresar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for hospital documentation: 'El paciente ingresó en la planta de cirugía ayer' (The patient was admitted to the surgical ward yesterday).

drenar.clinicalNote: Essential for describing fluid removal procedures: 'Drenamos el absceso quirúrgicamente' (We drained the abscess surgically).

referir.clinicalNote: Essential for medical contexts: 'Te refiero al cardiólogo' (I'm referring you to the cardiologist) or 'Se refiere a los síntomas' (It refers to the symptoms).

auscultar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for describing physical examination procedures; use imperativeFormal when instructing patients to breathe deeply during lung auscultation.

palpar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for physical examination; physicians use this to describe tactile assessment of organs and tissues.

presentYo.irregularNote: í-diaeresis required; stressed í in -iar verbs

presentTu.irregularNote: í-diaeresis required; stressed í in -iar verbs

presentEl.irregularNote: í-diaeresis required; stressed í in -iar verbs

presentEllos.irregularNote: í-diaeresis required; stressed í in -iar verbs

radiografiar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in radiology departments; use to order or describe radiographic imaging procedures in patient assessment and documentation.

analizar.clinicalNote: Essential in medical contexts: 'Necesitamos analizar los resultados de laboratorio' (We need to analyze the lab results).

medicar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for prescribing medications: 'Necesitamos medicar al paciente con antibióticos.'

recetar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for physicians: 'Le receto este medicamento tres veces al día' (I prescribe this medication three times daily).

hospitalizar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for admitting patients; use 'Vamos a hospitalizarlo' to inform families of admission decisions.

transfundir.clinicalNote: Essential medical term used to describe the administration of blood or blood products to a patient; commonly used in emergency, surgical, and hematology settings.

intubar.clinicalNote: Essential medical term for airway management; used in emergency, anesthesia, and critical care settings to describe placement of an endotracheal tube.

ventilar.clinicalNote: Essential in respiratory medicine: 'Necesitamos ventilar al paciente mecánicamente' (We need to mechanically ventilate the patient).

cateterizar.clinicalNote: Essential in cardiology and urology; use present tense for procedural explanations and preterite to document completed catheterizations in patient records.

desinfectar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb used in hospital and clinical protocols to describe sterilization procedures: 'Desinfecte el área quirúrgica antes de la intervención' (Disinfect the surgical area before the procedure).

esterilizar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in surgical and laboratory settings; used when instructing staff on instrument sterilization protocols.

amputar.clinicalNote: Essential surgical verb used in medical consultations and operative reports to describe limb removal procedures.

rehabilitar.clinicalNote: Essential in physical therapy and recovery contexts; used when describing treatment plans for patients regaining function after injury or illness.

fisioterapear.clinicalNote: Used by physiotherapists to document treatment sessions: 'Le fisioterapeo la lesión del hombro tres veces por semana.'

vacunar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb used to instruct patients on immunization protocols: 'Necesitamos vacunarle contra la gripe' (We need to vaccinate you against the flu).

inmunizar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for vaccination programs and immunology protocols: 'El pediatra inmuniza a los niños contra el sarampión.'

biopsiar.clinicalNote: Essential in oncology and pathology: 'El médico biopsió el tejido sospechoso para confirmar el diagnóstico.'

endoscopiar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for gastroenterologists and surgeons documenting procedures: 'El médico endoscopió al paciente para evaluar la úlcera gástrica.'

ecografiar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for radiologists and sonographers: 'Voy a ecografiarle el abdomen' (I'm going to perform an abdominal ultrasound on you).

extraer.clinicalNote: Essential verb for describing surgical procedures: 'El cirujano extrajo la muela' (The surgeon extracted the tooth) or 'Necesitamos extraer sangre' (We need to draw blood).

incisionar.clinicalNote: Essential surgical verb used to describe making precise cuts during operative procedures; commonly used in surgical reports and operative instructions.

irrigar.clinicalNote: Essential surgical term: 'Voy a irrigar la herida con solución salina' (I will irrigate the wound with saline solution).

comprimir.clinicalNote: Essential verb in medical practice for instructing patients to compress wounds, apply pressure, or during compression therapy procedures.

inmovilizar.clinicalNote: Essential in orthopedic and emergency medicine: 'Necesitamos inmovilizar la fractura antes de trasladar al paciente' (We need to immobilize the fracture before transferring the patient).

resecar.clinicalNote: Essential surgical verb used to describe tissue removal: 'El cirujano resecó el tumor maligno' (The surgeon resected the malignant tumor).

trasplantar.clinicalNote: Essential surgical term used when discussing organ or tissue transplantation procedures with patients and medical teams.

dializar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for nephrologists and dialysis technicians: 'El paciente se dializa tres veces por semana' (The patient undergoes dialysis three times per week).

oxigenar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for respiratory care: 'Necesitamos oxigenar al paciente inmediatamente' (We need to oxygenate the patient immediately).

sediar.clinicalNote: Used in surgical and pathology contexts to describe the localization or encapsulation of infection, abscess, or pathological process within a defined anatomical region.

anestesiar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for anesthesiologists and surgeons to describe administering anesthesia to patients before procedures.

desfilar.clinicalNote: Used in medical contexts to describe patient processions during hospital rounds or the sequential examination of patients in clinical settings.

sangrar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb for describing bleeding symptoms; use in patient assessments, wound care, and emergency protocols—e.g., 'El paciente sangra abundantemente' (The patient is bleeding heavily).

vomitar.clinicalNote: Essential in medical assessment: use to ask patients about nausea and vomiting symptoms, documented frequency, and associated conditions.

agonizar.englishParallel: to be dying; to be in agony
agonizar.clinicalNote: Essential in palliative care and end-of-life documentation to describe a patient's final moments or acute distress.

reinfundir.clinicalNote: Used in transfusion and infusion protocols to describe the process of administering fluids or blood products back into a patient's circulatory system.

dilatar.clinicalNote: Essential in ophthalmology and cardiology; used to describe pupil dilation, blood vessel expansion, or cervical dilation during labor.

hipertensar.clinicalNote: Médicos utilizan este verbo para describir factores que elevan la presión arterial en pacientes.

estabilizar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for describing patient stabilization: 'Estabilizamos al paciente después de la intervención quirúrgica.'

monitorizar.clinicalNote: Essential in medical settings: 'Monitorizamos los signos vitales del paciente durante la cirugía' (We monitored the patient's vital signs during surgery).

infiltrar.clinicalNote: Essential in oncology and rheumatology: 'Infiltramos anestésico local en la zona' (We infiltrated local anesthetic in the area).

aspirar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb used when performing aspiration procedures, such as needle aspiration or airway suctioning: 'Voy a aspirar la secreción' (I'm going to aspirate the secretion).

laparoscopiar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in surgical contexts for describing minimally invasive diagnostic or therapeutic procedures performed with a laparoscope.

tomar.clinicalNote: Essential for medication instructions: 'Tome dos pastillas cada ocho horas' (Take two pills every eight hours).

controlar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for patient care: 'Necesitamos controlar su presión arterial' (We need to monitor your blood pressure) or 'El paciente controla su diabetes bien' (The patient manages his diabetes well).

administrar.clinicalNote: Essential verb for medical practice: 'Se administra la medicación intravenosa' (The medication is administered intravenously).

curar.clinicalNote: Essential medical verb for treatment outcomes; commonly used in phrases like 'curar una herida' (to heal a wound) or 'curar una enfermedad' (to cure a disease).

vendarar.clinicalNote: Essential verb used to instruct patients: 'Vendare la herida cada día' (Bandage the wound daily).

enfermar.englishParallel: to fall ill / to become sick
enfermar.clinicalNote: Used to describe the onset or development of illness: 'El paciente enfermó hace dos semanas' (The patient became ill two weeks ago).

instalar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in construction: 'Instalamos la tubería ayer' (We installed the piping yesterday). Use present progressive for ongoing work: 'Están instalando los cables eléctricos' (They are installing the electrical wires).

soldar.clinicalNote: Essential construction term: 'El obrero suelda las vigas metálicas con precisión' (The worker welds the metal beams precisely).

demoler.clinicalNote: Essential in construction contexts: 'Vamos a demoler el edificio antiguo' (We are going to demolish the old building).

nivelar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb for describing the process of making surfaces even or leveling ground and structures during building and site preparation work.

excavar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb for describing earthmoving operations: 'Los obreros excavan los cimientos' (The workers are excavating the foundations).

rellenar.englishParallel: to fill in / to fill up
rellenar.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for directing workers to fill cavities, cracks, or gaps with mortar, concrete, or other materials.

amarrar.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for securing materials, equipment, and loads; commonly used in safety protocols: 'Amarra bien las cuerdas antes de izar la carga.'

impermeabilizar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in construction: 'Necesitamos impermeabilizar la azotea antes de las lluvias' (We need to waterproof the roof before the rains).

inspeccionar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in construction for quality control: 'El ingeniero inspecciona los cimientos antes de continuar con la obra.'

cementar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb used to describe bonding materials with cement in masonry and structural work applications.

engrasar.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for lubricating machinery and equipment; workers must engrasar regularly to prevent mechanical failure.

tornillar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb for fastening materials with screws; commonly used in command forms on job sites: 'Tornilla esta placa al marco.'

atornillar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in construction: 'Atornilla los paneles al marco con cuidado' (Screw the panels to the frame carefully).

taladrar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb: 'Necesitamos taladrar agujeros en la pared antes de instalar los anclajes' (We need to drill holes in the wall before installing the anchors).

cortar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb for instructing workers to cut materials, trim edges, or reduce dimensions on-site.

lijar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb for finishing work; use with protective equipment: 'Lija la superficie antes de pintar' (Sand the surface before painting).

pintar.clinicalNote: Essential construction verb for specifying painting tasks: 'Pinta las paredes de blanco' or 'Necesitamos pintar antes del viernes.'

sellar.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for describing waterproofing operations: 'Sellamos las grietas antes de aplicar el acabado' (We seal the cracks before applying the finish).

imperativeFormal.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

imperativeInformal.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

presentYo.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

presentTu.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

presentEl.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

presentEllos.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

subjunctiveEl.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

subjunctiveTu.irregularNote: i→í stem change in stressed syllable

aislar.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for isolating work zones, materials, or thermal/acoustic sections during building projects.

encofrar.englishParallel: to formwork / to frame with scaffolding
encofrar.clinicalNote: Essential in Spanish construction terminology for describing the installation of formwork (cofra) around concrete structures during building projects.

verter.englishParallel: to pour, to spill, to dump
verter.clinicalNote: Essential in construction contexts: 'Vierte el concreto en el molde' (Pour the concrete into the mold) is a fundamental instruction on job sites.

vibrar.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for describing vibrating machinery, compactors, and equipment oscillation during work operations.

compactar.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for describing soil compaction, asphalt compression, and material consolidation on job sites.

medir.clinicalNote: Essential in construction for specifying dimensions: 'Mide la altura de la pared' (Measure the wall height).

imperativeFormal.irregularNote: c→qu before e to preserve /k/ sound

subjunctiveEl.irregularNote: c→qu before e to preserve /k/ sound

subjunctiveTu.irregularNote: c→qu before e to preserve /k/ sound

preteriteYo.irregularNote: c→qu before e to preserve /k/ sound

marcar.clinicalNote: Essential for construction work: 'Marca las líneas de corte con precisión' (Mark the cut lines precisely) or 'Hemos marcado los puntos de referencia' (We have marked the reference points).

trazar.englishParallel: to trace, to draw, to layout
trazar.clinicalNote: Essential verb in construction for describing how to sketch plans, outline routes, or mark building layouts on site.
